column3: Subtitle
Eating all my meals at subway for a full day.
Let's kick things off with their new breakfast sandwiches.
I went with their sausage and egg breakfast sandwich.
And they're totally customizable,
by the way, so I can get as much toppings and sauces I want.
Breakfast is the most important meal of the day.
For lunch, I'm going with their shawarma chicken rice bowl.
It's super filling, perfect for keeping me going.
It's three PM you know what that means.
It's snack time. This calls for the new 4 inch snack,
which is, I had to go with the Turkey ranch,
small but seriously tasty.
For dinner, you can't go wrong with their Thundury twist footlong
from their globally inspired subway series,
cold flavors all the way. And of course,
we're ending the day off with a footlong cookie for dessert.
Subway's got you covered all day long.
Swing by your local spot and grab what you're craving today.",21600000,64500,1743,3618,Subway,"34 Church St, Toronto, ON M5E 1T3, Canada",0.85737747891,1

column3: Subtitle
In my frozen ramen kit collection,
I only have 2 frozen ramen kits left.
This one is called the Gyo Kai Tonkotsu Ramen
and it has a seafood and pork bone based broth flavor.
I'm already excited
when I saw how rich and creamy that broth look.
You get a gigantic slab of chasu.
I wish it was a bit more fatty though.
But what blows my mind is the broth.
Ultra creamy and rich from the pork
and they steep it with bonito flakes to get that deep
seafood flavor.
This is probably my favorite flavor out of the 6 so far,
but I'm kind of sad because after this one
I only have one frozen ramen kit left.",16300000,573500,6223,34700,Ikkousha Ramen Toronto,"249 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1Z4, Canada",0.55634771282,4

if you're looking for a unique date idea
let me introduce you to Canada's largest trampoline park
by the way this account is for people who wanna have the best summer ever
I'd vlog fun activities every day
so don't forget to follow along
upla just opened up and it's an hour outside of Toronto
and it's so worth the drive because this place is insanely fun
the entire course is made out of trampoline nets

just make sure you don't fall over
because you will get launched into the air if someone jumps next to you
they also have these rope slides which are so fun
as you can see
my boyfriend has no care in the world
me and my brother are a little bit more sensible
and they had dodgeball
this is probably our favorite part
we had so much fun just jumping around
they also have this other section
where there's this rope tube that you can climb through to get to the top
and once you're up, it's a little scary
but they have huge bean bags there that you can just chill on
this is honestly such a fun experience
I definitely recommend you guys check this place out
and don't forget to follow along for more fun activities in the GTA",9300000,1300000,123200,250600,Crepe It Up,"91 Front St E, Toronto, ON M5E 1C3, Canada",1.04074508773,2

column3: Subtitle
I'm gonna let my boyfriend be a passenger
Prince for once.
He does so much for me.
So I wanted to plan an adventurous date for him
filled with activities and food that I knew he'd love.
I, of course,
was gonna be driving us everywhere.
So I started off by picking him up in my new sweet ride
Nissan Pathfinder.
The first stop was an indoor trampoline park.
He definitely likes being super active and adventurous,
so I knew he'd love this so much.
We both honestly had such a good time,
but we also worked up a huge appetite,
which was perfect because next up,
we're driving to his favorite sushi spot.
Drive is long,
so we don't do it often,
but these comfy seats with toasty seat warmers,
wireless charging
and blindspot warnings
make the drive seem shorter than it is.
We jammed out to his favorite songs,
which sounds so good
thanks to the 12 speakers in this car.
And we parked with ease using the Aroundview mirror.
Before we knew it,
we were here enjoying some amazing sushi.
Last but not least,
we went to go find the perfect Christmas tree.
This is actually gonna be the first Christmas
I'll be spending with his family
and since Christmas is super important to him,
I knew he'd really enjoy this activity.
After finding the perfect tree
we easily fitted into the car to bring home,
this was the perfect way to end off our day overall.
We had such a blast
and the Nissan Pathfinder was the perfect chariot
for my passenger prints.",9000000,32800,327,197,Ju Sushi & Bar,"383 Dundas St E, Toronto, ON M5A 2A6, Canada",1.35367652824,3

column3: Subtitle
everything I ate at Wingstop
Wingstop is still pretty new in Toronto's
my boyfriend has never been
so we just decided to get a bunch of stuff that we've never tried before
starting off with their honey garlic boneless chicken wings
dipping them in the Ranch is a must
these were so incredibly juicy and they were so crispy
it's such a classic flavor
and it will always hit
and the ranch makes it 10 times better
okay these were the mango habanero wings right
and I was a little skeptical at first
because the thought of a mango flavored wing just threw me off
but I was so wrong
because this ended up being my favorite thing that I tried today
it had a really nice spice to it
and it was sweet
10 out of 10
another thing I really liked was their corn
it was seasoned so nicely you guys
and of course I dipped it in the ranch cause why not
okay next up were these Korean cute chicken tenders right
they were completely coated in sauce
like it was beautiful
and that sauce had a nice heat to it
it was honestly delicious
probably my next favorite thing that I tried
also I really like their blue cheese
I think I liked it a little bit more than their ranch
next up we got their barbecue chicken tenders and their barbecue sauce slaps
I don't care what anybody says
for fries we got their Voodoo fries
my boyfriend said that this was his favorite thing that he tried
we also got their Buffalo fries
but I definitely prefer the Voodoo fries
the cheese sauce in there was just incredible
so if you're planning on going to Wingstop and you don't know what to order
definitely get their mango habanero wings
I'd also recommend the Korean Q flavor
and if you're trying to play it safe their honey garlic is amazing
quick disclaimer this is everything that we didn't finish
we took the rest home
I don't want anybody with an ed getting because trust me
I've been there
balance is definitely key
but I'd love to know what your go to order is at Wingstop
so let me know in the comments",7600000,620200,51000,56700,Wingstop,"432 Church St, Toronto, ON M5B 2A4, Canada",0.97967531245,4

column3: Subtitle
everything I ate at the Cheesecake Factory part 4
my boyfriend has never been here before so you know I had to put him on
we asked for all brown bread because we all know that that is the best
I eagerly waited for his reaction and you know what he said to me
he said that it's overrated
I dam near lost my mind
but then he came around so all was right in the world
for appetizers we got the deep fried Mac and cheese balls
you guys know I love these
they're ooey
they're gooey they're cheesy and they're crispy
I don't know how they do it but it is incredible
and it's the best appetizer in
in the world
if you've never had this before you are seriously missing out
some of my comments in my last video told me to get the egg roll sampler
and thank you very much because this was delicious
we ended up subbing out the cheeseburger one for an extra avocado roll
because those are incredible
and the sauce that it comes with is the best
the other 2 egg rolls in here had chicken in them and they were delicious
I really like the chicken taquito one
but if you can only get one appetizer
I would still go with the deep fried Mac and cheese balls
for our mains I had to get the orange chicken
we were honestly so full at this point
but one thing about me
if the food is in front of me i'mma eat it
and the orange chicken is incredible
it's so crispy and not too sweet
for dessert before you guys attack me in the comments again
we did get cheesecake this time
this was the tiramisu one
and it was so good
the next time I go I'm gonna try all new things
so let me know what your go to order is

169fff36-f5e8-412d-9d94-a79717fd8de6,fcbf5899-35e4-4e48-a036-e8bb42bf1faa,18f16288-9e8f-42f3-8a4a-7a90a7e53dfe,darnitdamon,https://www.tiktok.com/@darnitdamon/video/7309670500102458667,"red lobster endless shrimp!!! final ranking at the end #redlobster #endlessshrimpdeal ","I went to Red Lobster to have their 27 dollar endless shrimp deal, which apparently they lost 11 million dollars because they underestimated how much shrimp people can eat and I'm not sorry for contributing to that because it was so worth it. You start with the unlimited cheddar baby biscuits which I used to love as a kid and then my friends and I basically ordered all 8 shrimp options to try everything. The garlic shrimp scampe is a safe option you can't mess this up and same goes for the Wall Trim which is basically battered butterfly shrimp. The grilled shrimp skewer was seared to perfection, it was perfectly cooked. The shrimp offrido was good for approximately 1 bite and you realized you shouldn't be wasting some space on cream and pasta. Honestly I was pretty shocked at how fresh the shrimp was and nothing was overcooked at all but this first round was so easy. I think we collectively probably had like 64 shrimp so we had to keep going. Next was one of my favorite Steve crispy dragon shrimp which is sweet and a little bit spicy from the chili sauce but after that we collectively agreed that the coconut shrimp was in a bombination, it's so sweet and can only be salvaged with some hot sauce. And lastly to kill a lime shrimp kind of just hates to like a grilled shrimp skewer but I'm not complaining and I think I put away 32 shrimp so I'm pretty proud of myself but here's my fan ranking. Ok bye.",4000000,410000,15200,24200,Red Lobster,"790 Burnhamthorpe Rd W, Mississauga, ON L5C 3X3, Canada",23.43411192031,1

It's not that pizza doesn't exist in other cities, it's just not the same. This is Joe's, and I'd be lying if I said this my first time. The crust is always paper thin and usually slightly burnt. The sauce is more sweet than tangy, and the cheese is always just the right amount. When I think of the best pizza in New York, I think of Joe's. Not because it's actually the best, but because somewhere along the way of countless lane visits after a broken noise-hear-bar crawl in New York Square or post-grat spiral in Williamsburg, it is managed to occupy a little space in my heart. The next place is C-Mail, a newer fast casual spot serving affordable and Napoleon style pizza. I got the Trey Gouste to try three flavors. The pasto is the most fragrant, the cajuo pepe, which is the most decadent with the perfect amount of black pepper and the margaradeir, which is the classic. And there's one right next to the entrance of the High Line, one of my favorite spots in the city, so you can grab it to go and go people watch on the High Line. The last place is Scars, where the line is so long that they pass out garlic, not to the snack, and by the time they order, they're only Sicilian slices. I am slightly disappointed, but it does continue the street repeated ever seeing this video, and I can never say no to high quality for cajuo disguises pizza. I will miss New York City pizza.",1900000,310300,2161,17000,Joes Pizza and Pasta House Since 1967,"51 McMurchy Ave S, Brampton, ON L6Y 1Y5, Canada",30.72995975252,2

On weekdays, they're having crab legs with butter and steak served with chimichurri sauce. You never really eat your money's worth at a buffet, but just the steak and crab leg alone, I feel like it makes us super worth it. On weekends, their menu is even better with lobster tail, steamed scallop, and abalone with chicken pot. I'm always blown away by the variety I drag and purl buffet. The peak induct station is definitely iconic and a personal favorite. Their dim sum bar is always loaded with so many shallong balls, shoomais, and I noticed that they keep their food really fresh and always refill the food very quickly. There's also a really good variety of different food stations like their grill, grilling a pineapple, steak, chicken. The sushi bar was insane, so many variety of rolls into shimmy and classics like a soup station and bread station. Their dessert bar was just insane. They also have really great mocktails and drinks that were so refreshing. I went on a weekday with my mom and it was super duper packed so make sure to make a reservation because people were lining up. Honestly, I'm super sad that this is their last weekend for their Christmas menu, but if you have a chance, this is definitely worth checking out. Just look at the tongue-who-lose, crumb-rule, fruits, ice cream. Honestly, this is a foodies heaven. If you haven't been to drag and purl buffet yet, it lives up to the hype.",1600000,72400,14300,13400,Small Pot Restaurant,"155 Consumers Rd, North York, ON M2J 1W6",13.94296908693,1

a3d8422c-8239-4fb7-8069-86f31f602dbd,1510df83-6021-43a8-9f27-a53bf50f2856,0f4cba8a-18ee-4296-82ee-8ca23926b4a8,snackeatingsnacks,https://www.tiktok.com/@snackeatingsnacks/video/7489486877163031839,"We need some of these desserts back in McDonalds in the US! #mcdonalds #mcdonaldsbrasil #brazil #mequis ","I just tried McDonald's in Brazil and they're doing something here better than any other McDonald's in the entire world. So in Brazil, they have a lot of unique items that haven't come across anywhere else. And of course, I got a huge assortment to try, which includes the best McDonald's desserts ever that you need to know about. But first, let's start with the specialty burgers. I ordered something called the Bravo Clubhouse, which was supposed to have show you grilled onions and Edmonton cheese, but instead they gave me the Bravo Melt Onion Ring Burger by mistake. It has bacon, onion rings, cheese sauce, but the bacon and onion rings were all soggy and it was just a miss. Next, I got the Beef Tasty Turbo, which is something called Tasty Sauce and a lot of it. It's garlicy, smoky, and creamy, and it's definitely deserving of the name Tasty Sauce. Next, McDonald's Brazil has a sauce called Kaipita. I tried it with some fries and it's kind of like a smoky barbecue, but a bit more vinegary. But the real place that Brazil McDonald's shines is definitely with their desserts. They have a full chocolate ice cream cone, which was awesome. And I like how they add these little fudge filled cookies on top. They have something called a mixed dip, which is like ice cream nachos. So you get these little waffles and you can dip into the caramel and hot fudge and M&Ms. So fun. But my favorite thing in Mickey D's Brazil is this oval teen rock smock flurry, which is super popular here. It's like a malt shake with lots of chocolate and these little chocolate covered cereal things. They kind of reminded me of a bunch of crunch. Now if you're a millennial, you'll know what that is. But I got a lot more McDonald's reviews coming around the world, so make sure to follow for more food adventures.",1300000,125700,15700,7724,McDonald's,"1256 Eglinton Ave W, Mississauga, ON L5M 2B5, Canada",25.66763412362,3

Today I'm super excited to be trying out my Rayman Meta glasses to help me capture some hands-free footage, while picking out some very fun foods in the grocery store. Pats a grapis, Earl Grey Milk Tea, before we did any shopping. I love this Asian grocery store, especially going through the snack aisle. They always have something so fun and exotic or very nostalgic at least. I feel like the food is way more fresh here and there's so many options, especially for seafood. I was so excited to find this viral fruit ice cream. I was in the sizes about which flavor to get, but mangoes always go through for me. I really love this grocery store too, because they have a whole selfie booth section in a food court. In a gaming section with some coin slides. I found a couple of fun food options to try. So let's rate them. First thing was this macro negiri. It was tasty, simple, and will make a perfect quick snack. I give this to eight out of 10. Next were these sweet potato chips. It tastes just like canned sweet potato. It had a nice salty sweetened balance. I give these to seven out of 10. Next I got these Tokoyaki flavor cheese puffs. They were airy crunchy and were full of a barbecue-like flavor. For me, I give this a six out of 10. And lastly was this viral mango shaped ice cream. This by far was my favorite pickup. It is not over height. The outside is like a white chocolate encasing. And the inside tastes just like a mango milk tea. It was a 10 out of 10. Overall today we had a fun mix. Let me know what I should try next. And huge thanks to my Rayban medical astas for helping me capture today's experience. Y'all should definitely check them out. This is such a fun accessory.",1200000,3027,188,256,Nations Fresh Foods • Mississauga Store,"2933-B Eglinton Ave W, Mississauga, ON L5M 6J3, Canada",29.36048866478,1

Honey butter toast, a proper fish and chips and an epic Indian feast! Amazing day of eats! #london #londoneats #londonrestaurants #londonfoodguide ","Here's what I in a day in London, first time or edition. Started the day at a bakery called Arom because I had to try their specialty. Something called a honey butter toast, which is made with Japanese milk bread and caramelized on the outside. But inside, pillowy soft in the corners, crispy and absolute perfection. Then for lunch, I popped over to the rocket soul place, a proper old-school chippy that's been frying fish for over 150 years. Fish and chips is one of my favorite foods, but only when it's done right. I got the fried cod, which was a huge piece of fish that broke apart nicely. Give a quick dip in the tartar sauce and just listen to the crunch. The fish was moist, perfectly stuck to the batter and delicious. Easy saying this is a must get when in London. And the chips, their thick cut, almost more like steak fries with the mashed potato we made up. Not my favorite kind of fry? Sorry, chip. Later for dinner, I went to the tamel print, a top Indian restaurant in the city. And when I saw them making fresh roti like this, I knew I was in for a treat. The chicken wings were next level. Might be the best wings I've ever had. The onion bhaji was like a crispy onion-pash brown. The special masala dosa, proper tasty. Everything here was just elite. No wonder it's such a tough reservation in the city. But the real stunner, the hot fluffy roti that pulled apart like cotton candy, especially dipping the paneer butter masala, that combo was unreal. And don't worry, I've got loads more coming, including trying London's many viral desserts, a baked potato restaurant, trying British snacks, and so much more. So make sure to follow for more food adventures, yeah?",1100000,64400,4252,13300,"Aroma of India - Authentic Indian, Indo-Hakka & Nepali Cuisine","590 Dundas St, London, ON N6B 1W8, Canada",167.18259755877,5

169fff36-f5e8-412d-9d94-a79717fd8de6,d477f5a5-640a-40dd-bc19-f73723f69b59,29654303-6d09-4875-8d24-beed5618cdbb,darnitdamon,https://www.tiktok.com/@darnitdamon/video/7353449816153738527,"how is dungeness crab season almost over??? #whatieatinaday #dungenesscrab #mukbang #sffoodie ","My favorite time of the year, Dungeon is Crab Season is ending in like a week, so it's your last chance to go to the supermarket and get them for like 899 a pound. One thing I learned the hard ways, if you want to reheat it, steam with the head side down so the crab fat doesn't leak out. Breaking down the crab is kinda intimidating the first time, but once you get the hang of it, it's very easy. One of my favorite ways to eat it is with some rice, some butter, and then a lot of black pepper and lemon. This is the final result, and it's so creamy and rich, and it tastes like luxury. It definitely tastes like the ocean, so if that's not your vibe, I totally get it. I, however, have an entire crab to myself every two weeks during Dungeon is Crab Season, so I'm basically an expert. For the rest of the crab, and this part is a labor of love, if you find someone who's willing to do this for you, they're a keeper, you have to take out all the meat and mix it into some rice, and this process used to take me like 40 minutes, but given how much I've practiced my free time, I can do this in like 15 minutes now. Dungeon is Crab is also naturally sweet, so when you add in the butter mixture, and I forgot that I add in sesame oil too, but this becomes the most indulgent rice you've ever had. Dungeon is Crab Season starts in January and it's ending 8.8, so this is the last weekend to get your friends. Go to the supermarket, pick up some crabs, and have a Dungeon is Crab party, and it's the best weekend activity you can't just remind. Okay, bye.",1100000,60600,2782,5284,Seau de Crabe Seafood Restaurant,"670 Eglinton Ave W #4, Mississauga, ON L5R 3V2",23.96146651759,3

For my meat I got orange chicken and Beijing beef. It's regularly $10 for the plate but on the app I did have a coupon for $8. The orange chicken was so delicious, sticky, tangy, sweet and perfect. No wonder this is their number one dish for a good reason. The Beijing beef was pretty good. It has a nice spice and good flavor to it. The fried rice is always very mid to me but I really love their chow mein noodles. Did you know the most haunted house in Ontario
is located in Mississauga?
Cherry Hill House was built on Dundas and Copper Road
back in 1822 by a war veteran.
Joseph Silverthorn
was a soldier who fought in the war of 1812
and lived in the house with his wife and 12 children.
When the youngest Silverthorn,
George, was celebrating his marriage at home,
there's a knock at the door
and George went to answer it.
Some claim there was a mysterious visitor outside,
but it was too difficult to make out who it was.
After this, George was never to be seen again
and no trace of him was ever found.
Some say that
if you ever discuss his disappearance at the house,
doors would open and close on their own.
To this day.
Now, this isn't even the scariest thing to happen
at this house.
Go to part 2 to hear more
behind the horrors of the house.",636900,30600,6123,4543,Cherry Hill House,"Mississauga, ON L5A 3Z1, Canada",18.92969754742,5

Putting you on to actual hidden gems in Toronto.
And here's how to find this one.
If you're looking for your next weekend cafe
to hit up this summer,
save this video.
It's located in the back alleyway behind ST.
Clair Avenue West and Christie.
And to find it only to walk west towards the McDonald's
and make a right.
You'll know you're going in the right direction
when you spot this pink sign.
Follow it down this laneway.
Yes, I know it's a bit sketchy,
but it's there until you reach this pink window counter.
It's impossible to miss.
The menu here has a little bit of everything,
but there are some things that I really enjoyed
when I visited a couple times.
Their peanut butter banana smoothie slaps,
their rugelized on point.
And their maple tahini banana muffin is amazing.
This place is only open from Thursday to Sunday
with limited benches and outback.",589100,60300,8001,20200,Becked Goods Baked Goods Cafe,"684 St Clair Ave W, Toronto, ON M6C 1B1",4.67297941571,2

column3: Subtitle
Putting you on to ACTUAL hidden gems in Toronto
and here's how to find this one.
At the corner of this intersection in this residential part of a Etobicoke
you'll find this plaza.
Now it may not look like much,
but if you head inside of this Eastern European grocery store
you'll find one of Toronto's BEST hidden gems.
Now when you walk in you're going to want to walk all the way to the back,
past all the pantry items near the dairy section
and you're going to want to hang a left.
Make that left and you'll end up at this counter.
It may not look like much,
but this back section is home to some of the best cevapi in the city.
And if you don't know what those are
they're basically mini Eastern European sausages.
This place also makes fire burek as well if you're into that stuff.
This is me waiting for my food and here it is.
Now for under $10 you get 5 cevapi on a bun white onions and a drink.
There’s just something about these little guys
cooked over an open fire that make them so damn juicy.
It’s places like this that makes some of Toronto's best food.
You just need to know where to find it.
Episode 2, next week!",370500,27200,3115,5595,Mrakovic Fine Foods Etobicoke,"44 Wellesworth Dr, Etobicoke, ON M9C 4R1",15.83833196307,4

Basically when I was in her form, my mom suffered with depression, she was not happy with her life. She also really wanted me to be a boy. She is two boys now and she's really connected with them, but she was really upset that I was a girl. So when I was four, she decided that she wanted to leave. So one day she decided to buy plain tickets to her family in India and she dropped me in the parking lot of my dad's restaurant. She didn't even walk me in. She just dropped me in the parking lot and said go in, tell your dad I'm going to India and then she left. My dad's cook saw me standing outside and he decided to bring me in and my dad just assumed that she missed a walk to me in and he's and I was trying to tell him I'm like, dad, mommy said that she's going to India, she's going to India and he's like pleading to marry me because I'm four and I don't know what I'm talking about. And so after work, he decided to take me home and when we got home, that's when my dad saw. Her clothes were gone, bags were packed, like things were all gone. There was emergency cash she had, that was gone, her passport was gone. So she knew that she had left. And at that time, he was filled with anger. I remember he took one of those landlines, ripped it out of the wall and smacked it against the wall next to us. And at that point, he decided that he had to go back to his ex-wife because we're Indian and he is worried about how people would see him as a single dad with a child. So he took me to my grandmother's and said, can you take her? And my grandmother took me in. And then my dad went back to my, back to his ex-wife's wife who he had a child with and he stayed with her. And basically, once my grandma was, once I was with my grandma, I was seeing my mom here and there. She ended up coming back to Canada. And it was nice. It was actually the time that we bonded the most because she would try to make a day of fun for us. So she would take me to like the park or to like an amusement park, things like that. And I actually feel like I was bonding with her. So when I was seven, my grandma ended up dying. She got hit by a car. And after that, I had to go live with my stepmom who did not like me. She hated my mom and she did not want me there. And so basically, I ended up messaging my mom on Facebook. And because I wanted to make sure she was okay, I really missed her. And my stepmom saw that. And that was the day when she got extremely angry at me, hated me and ignored me for the rest of my life. She wanted the worst for me. And that's when it started. After that, me and my mom were just connected and talked like every year or so through the phone.",348900,9503,660,758,Dad's authentic Indian Kitchen,"100 Marmora St, North York, ON M9M 2X5, Canada",13.74056211402,1

[00:39-00:41] And the cranberries just a subtle sweetness. [00:43-00:44] I'm so impressed. [00:44-00:46] The char could look a little bit better though. [00:50-00:51] What even is stuffing? [00:51-00:54] If like me or too lazy to cook a holiday dinner, this will save you. [00:54-00:56] Thank you all for such a great 2025. [00:56-00:58] The Christmas pizza gets an 8.1. [00:58-01:00] But I can't wait to review Dave's hot chicken in 2026. And this is something that I absolutely love and was wanting to experiment with. Shallot Zhao. And I'm going to show you how I've made it. So first, I go in with ginger, the ends of green onions, a shit ton of shallots, lemon grass, a little bit of garlic, salt, pepper, of course, and Thai chilies. I go ahead and fold that up in some aluminum foil. And I'm going to sit this directly on the fire. Now I'm going to go ahead and flip this a couple of times. I turn on my stove to 375 and I'm going to finish this in the oven until my aromatic becomes soft. Now in my mortar, I'm going to go ahead and add my aromatic in. I'm going to put my pestle, I'm going to pound it out and it's going to look a little something like this. Now I'm going to go ahead and add in some cilantro, some green onions, and we're going to keep pounding. And now it's time to season it. So we're going to add in some fish sauce, some lime juice, get that a nice stir. I grated palm sugar and I use about four of these little ones. I give that a nice stir again and now I'm going to add in a little bit of salt. Y'all, this tamarind soup packet is freaking magic. We're going to go ahead and give that a stir and the reason why I'm stirring so much is because I don't want the clumps. Now I'm going to add in some homemade rice powder. Give that another stir. I also added a little bit of white pepper. Y'all, this is some bomb assed jowl. Period.",260100,24500,430,5698,Thai Cuisine Experts,"3960 Grand Park Dr #2, Mississauga, ON L5B 4M6, Canada",22.8297748943,2

It's the most famous Asian food fest in the US. Last year we sampled so many dishes they have over 300 varieties of food. Here are some sweet and savory dishes to look out for. The most refreshing come-quot sugar cane juice that you can watch being pressed fresh right in front of you. The Hawaiian honeycombs are one of the best bites of the experience we were in shot. The giant squid skewers are definitely a must try perfect to walk around with. And the tangulu candied fruit is so iconic this dessert went viral for a reason. It's so satisfying to eat. The steamed bow buns are so soft and even come with a vegan option. The purple yam ice cream was a favorite. It sounds weird but the flavors worked so well together. And it's such a good texture. This is happening on April 3rd to 5th in Atlantic Station. You can expect over 80 food vendors. It also has Asian cultural performances, a giant pandan plaitable, and a panda bounce house for kids. I started the week off strong by eating hot chicken from Loveburn. Their fried chicken sandwiches here are huge and juicy. Then on Friday, I headed over to Sausolito to show the carnage. It's a Brazilian steakhouse that does all you can eat meat and sushi. And the sushi here was so fresh. They had so many different roles and I love that they had a lot of salmon. And their pork ribs were too die-four. Look at the amount it had of meat on my plate. Then I headed over to sushi obsession on Saturday. They do roles here like no other. And this is the new foodie sushi queen role that you can now get. I love deep fried rolls. I am a sucker for it. And this Doug Roll deep fried was so good. Sushi obsession is also known for their sushi nachos, which are so good I had to get some to go. And they also do alcoholic and non-alcoholic boba drinks. This was their strawberry pina colada. On Saturday evening, I went to a caught your prairie cooking class by the feed app. This was taught by Chef Anna. She is a Ukrainian chef, but she taught us how to make a Georgian dish. You've probably seen this dish all over social media. It looks like a cheese boat. And it had butter, eggs, and cheese in it. It was scrumptious. Then on Sunday, my family and I went to Tancadilla, Aesteka, in Dublin. They do Asian fusion Mexican food here. We got their Agua Chile, which is my favorite. Fabidia and their Mexican pizza.",241900,6721,1584,3345,Kokoria - Korean fried chicken(Sushi in Sushi),"2310 Battleford Rd Unit #5, Mississauga, ON L5N 3K6, Canada",29.85475808817,2

First off, it's a very spacious in here with three different areas as well as cultural seating. There's beautiful photography, nature, vibrant colors and gorgeous touches. Now let's get into this amazing food spread. We had this amazing haidaise drink which is like a mango drink with a vinto syrup. It's so good. This strawberry drink which I loved as well. They offer amazing breakfast like this well-seasoned egg shakshuka, this liver which was so soft and this mixed meat packed with flavor. And it's amazing for studio with eggs and the bread was also great. They also have this iconic Yemeni dish called Fahsa which melts in your mouth. And Salta which basically tastes like a vegetable soup. Then we have these five amazing dishes all on top of this rice including the slow cooked buttery soft honey. Well-made juicy chicken mendi, this charred flavor packed well-seasoned chicken mutton, this tender lamb mendi and also fish which was also delicious. They're also working on getting more sauces and rice options as they open. We ended it off with dessert. These amazing fecta, we had the date one which was moist and flavorful. And the banana one which tasted like a really moist banana bread. I was obsessed with both of these. I definitely recommend Azuma. It's a great spot with delicious Yemeni food. Make sure to check it out ASAP.",127500,2874,1220,995,Sanaa AlYemen Restaurant,"2121 Dundas St E Unit 1, Mississauga, ON L4X 1M3, Canada",15.2256745331,4

And don't mind me, I know there are many more of you guys out there, okay? I'm out of cram and sanwase and holy sweet treat heaven. Immediately got started with this three-fruit yogurt smoothie, your Vietnamese and I've tried ya' ul. That is literally what it tastes like, it is so nostalgic to my childhood. Starry, creamy, delicious, refreshing, also tried their ube coconut coffee which was strong and their banana cream matcha before digging into their food. Yes, I am that person that orders popcorn chicken every establishment and I don't care, it's good. And yes, they also have venyais. Many personally I like the normal ones, but if you like a slightly more bitter taste, go with the matcha ones. They're warm, piping hot, fluffy, but we gotta get into these mochi donuts. You know you can get mochi donuts everywhere and there's not much that sets apart, but these have to be my favorite, they have a variety of flavors, they're generous with the coating on top and they have the perfect chew. For anyone that was curious, my favorite flavors were the pandan and ube. And you guys, we are not done yet because look at this egg puff with ice cream and fruit. And this gigantic crepe, love the contrast between the warm and fluffy crepe and egg puff, will be cold ice cream and the fruit to tie it all together. Save the best for last these malasadas, it's like biting into warm pillowy heaven with the most decadent cream filling. Thousand nine to ten you gotta check them out.",121700,9624,3505,3806,The Mango Place - Hui Lau Shan Mississauga Boba + Desserts,"521 Curran Place, Mississauga, ON L5B 0H4, Canada",22.55915417833,2
